items.title: Pas urges standardised disclosure of zakat fund management after RM230mil probe
items.summary: Caption: Pas calls for audits, transparency and institutional reform as public concern grows over alleged misuse of charitable funds. Its secretary-general, Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan, said the move was among key measures needed following the alleged misappropriation of RM230 million linked to charitable funds. Meanwhile, Takiyuddin said the case had sparked widespread public concern and drawn national attention.

items.title: Anwar offers prayers, congratulations to Sultan Mizan on 64th birthday
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 26 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim extended his highest congratulations to Sultan of Terengganu Sultan Mizan Zainal Abidin on the occasion of his 64th birthday today. Anwar, in a Facebook post, also prayed that Sultan Mizan and the Sultanah of Terengganu, Sultanah Nur Zahirah, as well as the entire royal family, be continually blessed with His mercy and guidance. Daulat Tuanku!” said Anwar.

items.title: Kelantan government denies involvement in airport motorcycle convoy
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The motorcycle convoy to Sultan Ismail Petra Airport (LTSIP) yesterday was an individual initiative and not organised by the Kelantan government, says Deputy Menteri Besar Datuk Dr Mohamed Fadzli Hassan. Fadzli said the state government had no involvement in the activity and stressed that all parties must comply with regulations, especially in public areas. "All parties must always adhere to the law and prioritise safety, particularly in sensitive locations such as airports.

items.title: Zambry: Keep higher‑education admissions merit‑based, free from politics
items.summary: TANJONG MALIM, April 26 — Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir expressed hope that student admissions into public higher education institutions (IPTAs) would no longer be politicised to the extent that it affects the fundamental principles and the students involved. Zambry said admissions into IPTAs are based on merit, and any issues that arise can be referred to the Unit Pusat Universiti (UPU). Also present were Perak Education, Higher Education, Youth and Sports Committee chairman Datuk Khairudin Abu Hanipah, and UPSI vice-chancellor Professor Datuk Dr Md Amin Md Taff.

items.title: Selangor exco slams viral image linking pig in royal attire to Sultan, urges police to act
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 26 — Selangor executive councillor Ng Sze Han today condemned a social media post showing an image of a pig dressed in royal ceremonial attire and linked it to the Sultan of Selangor. “I strongly condemn the action of a social media post which edited an image of a pig wearing palace ceremonial attire and linked it to His Royal Highness the Sultan of Selangor. Controversies involving pigs resurfaced after the Sultan of Selangor’s recent express disagreement with pig farming activities in the state.

items.title: MetMalaysia issues thunderstorm warning for 8 states until 7pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms and heavy rain affecting eight states until 7pm this evening. In a statement today, MetMalaysia said the severe weather is expected to hit Kelantan (Pasir Mas and Tanah Merah), Pahang (Jerantut and Maran) and Selangor (Kuala Selangor and Sepang) Negri Sembilan, Melaka and Johor (Tangkak, Segamat, Muar, Batu Pahat, Kluang, Pontian and Kulai) are also affected, In Sarawak, the affected areas are Kuching (Bau and Lundu), Sri Aman (Lubok Antu), Sarikei (Pakan, Julau and Meradong), Sibu, Mukah (Daro, Matu and Dalat) and Limbang. The same weather warning also applies to several areas in Sabah, namely Pedalaman (Sipitang, Beaufort, Nabawan and Keningau), Papar, Tawau (Tawau, Semporna and Lahad Datu), and Sandakan (Tongod and Kinabatangan).

items.title: Entrepreneur Development Ministry seeks feedback from industry players on West Asia conflict pressures, says minister
items.summary: KEPALA BATAS, April 26 — The Ministry of Entrepreneur and Cooperative Development (KUSKOP) is actively conducting engagement sessions with stakeholders, including micro, small and medium enterprise (MSME) associations, hawkers and cooperatives, to identify the impact of the West Asia conflict on the domestic economy. Minister Steven Sim said the move is important to assess the pressures faced by industry players for the government to formulate appropriate solutions. He said global conflicts, including economic tensions, tariff and trade disputes, as well as armed conflicts, are contributing to global economic uncertainty, affecting supply chains and in turn impacting the national economy.

items.title: High Court dismisses father’s negligence claim against UUM over son’s death by electrocution
items.summary: Judge Dr John Lee Kien How @ Mohammad Johan Lee ruled that the plaintiff, R. Sivakumar, 57, had failed to prove that electrocution was the cause of the death of his son, S. Vinosiny, 21. Therefore, the plaintiff’s claim is dismissed with costs,” he said, ordering Sivakumar to pay RM5,000 in costs to UUM. Lawyers M. Manoharan and M. Hariharan represented Sivakumar, while UUM was represented by lawyers P. Barvina and Y. Sathisharajah.

items.title: PAS sets bold Melaka goal in state election, minimum 15 out of 28
items.summary: MELAKA, April 26 — PAS is aiming to capture Melaka in the coming state election, with the floor target set at 15 of the 28 seats in total. “We see 24 seats as not too difficult. In the 2021 Melaka state election, Barisan Nasional (BN) won 21 seats, Pakatan Harapan secured five, and Perikatan Nasional – of which PAS is a component – took two.

items.title: Stranded humpback whale off Germany to be towed to North Sea
items.summary: WISMAR (Germany): A humpback whale stranded off the Baltic Sea island of Poel is set to be towed to the North Sea on a barge in a multi-day operation, organisers said on Saturday, reported German Press Agency (dpa). Felix Bohnsack, technical lead of the private initiative behind the plan, said the whale could be transported over about three and a half days past Fehmarn to waters near Skagen, more than 400 kilometres away. The plan is to transport the 12-tonne male whale using a barge.

items.title: Anwar extends birthday wishes to Sultan Mizan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim has extended his highest congratulations to the Sultan of Terengganu, Sultan Mizan Zainal Abidin, on the occasion of his 64th birthday today. In a Facebook post, Anwar also conveyed prayers for Sultanah Nur Zahirah and the entire Terengganu royal family, wishing them continued blessings, health and prosperity. Daulat Tuanku!"

items.title: Man's RM3mil negligence suit against UUM over daughter's death dismissed
items.summary: ALOR STAR: A man whose daughter died allegedly due to an electric shock at a public university hostel four years ago has failed in his bid to seek RM3 million in damages from the institution. High Court Judge Dr Mohammad Johan Lee ruled that the plaintiff, R. Sivakumar, 57, failed to prove negligence against Universiti Utara Malaysia (UUM). In his judgment, Johan said Sivakumar failed to establish that an electric shock was the primary cause of his daughter, Vinosiny's death.

items.title: MetMalaysia warns of thunderstorms, heavy rain in Sabah, Sarawak and four other states till evening
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 26 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds affecting six states until 5.00 pm this evening. In Sarawak, the affected areas are Sri Aman, Sarikei (Sarikei and Meradong), Sibu (Sibu), and Mukah (Tanjung Manis, Daro, Matu and Dalat). The same weather warning also applies to several areas in Sabah, namely Pedalaman (Nabawan), Tawau (Tawau, Semporna and Lahad Datu), and Sandakan (Kinabatangan).

items.title: 5 contractors slapped with DBKL compounds for flouting SOPs in road excavation works
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) issued eight compound notices to five contractors during a late-night enforcement operation targeting public road excavation works across the capital. DBKL said monitoring and inspections were conducted to assess ongoing works by contractors involving pipe installation, road resurfacing, drain repairs and other utility-related works such as water, electricity and telecommunications infrastructure. Among the offences detected were failure to install road blinker lights, absence of traffic controllers, damage to pedestrian walkways, unauthorised road closures, lorries operating without permits to transport construction materials, and causing public roads to become dirty.

items.title: Govt foils two diesel misappropriation attempts in Kuching
items.summary: KUCHING: The Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry Sarawak foiled two attempts to misappropriate diesel under Ops Statik in separate cases around Kuching yesterday. Its Sarawak director Matthew Dominic Barin said in the first case around 2pm, a Nissan Frontier was found in suspicious circumstances and believed to have been used to store and transport controlled goods, namely diesel, for illegal purposes. He said officers seized 160 litres of diesel worth RM344, along with 16 jerry cans and several documents related to diesel purchases.

items.title: Stop politicising higher education admissions, says Zambry
items.summary: TANJONG MALIM: Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir has called for an end to the politicisation of student admissions into public higher education institutions (IPTAs). Also present were Perak Education, Higher Education, Youth and Sports Committee chairman Datuk Khairudin Abu Hanipah and UPSI vice-chancellor Professor Datuk Dr Md Amin Md Taff. Zambry said further study opportunities were not limited to public universities, but included Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institutions, polytechnics, and community colleges.

items.title: Umno’s Dr Akmal readies for court fight after PAS leader’s ‘chess piece’ jab
items.summary: The criticism came amid ongoing political tensions and court cases involving DAP leaders, and Merlimau assemblyman Datuk Dr Akmal Saleh said he would not back down legally if required. Dr Akmal also accused Rembia assemblyman Datuk Muhammad Jailani Khamis of neglecting his state legislative duties, alleging attendance issues despite being present at political events while reportedly on medical leave. “Advise him not to skip assembly sittings,” Dr Akmal said.

items.title: Pas targets at least 15 seats in Melaka, sees potential for 21
items.summary: MELAKA: Pas has set a target of winning at least 15 out of 28 state seats in the next Melaka election, with its election director, Muhammad Sanusi Md Nor, expressing confidence in securing up to 21. Sanusi said the party viewed the majority of constituencies as winnable if the right strategy was applied. Sanusi, who is also Kedah Menteri Besar, said only four seats currently held by DAP were considered challenging, while the rest presented strong opportunities for the party.

items.title: Former Business Times editorial veteran Teoh Hock Siew dies
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Former Business Times sub-editor Teoh Hock Siew passed away yesterday after a battle with dengue. Former The New Straits Times Press group managing editor Mustapha Kamil Mohd Janor said Teoh was already part of the subs desk when he first joined the organisation. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: NSTP booth draws crowds as 300 rice packs snapped up in 10 mins
items.summary: BATU KAWAN: The New Straits Times Press (NSTP) booth drew a strong crowd after 300 one-kilogramme packs of rice were snapped up within 10 minutes during a corporate social responsibility (CSR) initiative here. News and Current Affairs (NCA), Television Network group editor Azhari Muhidin said the overwhelming response reflected the public's appreciation for such outreach efforts. He also said that Media Prima, through its various platforms including NSTP titles and broadcast networks, regularly organises CSR programmes to assist communities in need.

items.title: State had no role in airport convoy, Kelantan deputy MB says after backlash
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, April 26 — The motorcycle convoy to Sultan Ismail Petra Airport (LTSIP) here yesterday was not organised by the Kelantan state government, but was an individual initiative. Deputy Menteri Besar Datuk Dr Mohamed Fadzli Hassan said the state government had no involvement in the activity, stressing that all parties must comply with regulations, especially in public areas. — Bernama Recommended read: ‘Serves them right’: Kelantan rep boasts motorcycle convoy used ‘lorong tikus’ to evade police road blocks

items.title: Man on travel blacklist over Ampang surau azan petition
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: Police have placed a local man on the Special Circular List to prevent him from fleeing the country following a petition regarding the azan at a surau in Ampang. Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar said the measure followed reports lodged by residents and surau committee members. He said that so far, police have recorded statements from several individuals, including representatives of the local community affected by the issue, to help complete the investigation papers.

items.title: Trump's security again faces scrutiny after press dinner shooting
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The shooting on Saturday night of a Secret Service agent at the White House Correspondents' Association dinner raises questions yet again about the protection afforded to America's political leaders at a time of increased political violence. Hundreds of agents from several law enforcement agencies were tasked with protecting the annual bash, which President Donald Trump headlined this year. During an impromptu White House press conference after the incident, Trump praised first responders, including the Secret Service.

items.title: Police debunk viral crime claims in Johor
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI: Police dismissed WhatsApp messages alleging a gang was involved in robbery, murder, and gang rape in Bandar Penawar. District police chief Superintendent Yusof Othman said the claims were false. "No such incidents were reported, including claims that a body was found in a drain near the National Youth Skills Institute in Bandar Penawar.

items.title: Jerantut retiree’s home burns down after husband dies, son’s wedding dreams go up in flames
items.summary: JERANTUT, April 26 — A retiree in Kampung Bukit Genting is facing a double tragedy after her home was destroyed in a fire just 10 days after the death of her husband. When I checked, the fire was already raging and had started burning stored old items like rags. Her son, Mohamad Hairi Saadun, said the family accepts the tribulation despite losing almost everything in the fire.

items.title: Police complete Mais defamation probe, papers with AGC for action, says Selangor top cop
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 26 — Police have submitted investigation papers on alleged defamatory claims made via TikTok against the Selangor Islamic Religious Council (Mais) to the Attorney General’s Chambers (AGC) for further action. Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar said statements had been recorded from the individual involved, who was identified as having links to a non-governmental organisation. Earlier, police were investigating an individual suspected of making defamatory statements against Mais and the Selangor Islamic Religious Department (Jais) through social media posts related to a mosque project in Subang.

items.title: Immigration foils RM3,500‑per person migrant smuggling using fake stamps, Hat Yai transit
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 26 — The Malaysian Immigration Department (JIM) foiled an attempt to smuggle Indonesian migrants using layered travel routes and fake entry stamps, following a special operation early yesterday. Its director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said 11 individuals, including a suspected ‘transporter’, were detained in the operation carried out by officers from the Anti-Trafficking in Persons and Anti-Smuggling of Migrants Act 2007 (ATIPSOM) and the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act 2001 (AMLATFPUAA) units at JIM headquarters, Putrajaya. Zakaria said at TBS, three men and three women, also Indonesian nationals, were detained for suspected links to the same syndicate.

items.title: Eurovision, venerable institution where art meets politics
items.summary: THE Eurovision Song Contest is famous for celebrating everything from cross-dressing ballad singers to heavy metal bands in monster masks, but geopolitics is threatening its 70th anniversary edition. More than 1,000 artists this week urged a boycott, and several countries have already said they are staying away in protest over Israel's participation. Five broadcasters, including Spain, are expected to boycott the 2026 edition over Israel's participation.

items.title: BSFL, potential chicken feed to reduce dependence on import
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The country's poultry industry is still seen as overly dependent on imported feed sources, thus exposing it to the risk of global market volatility including price increases and supply disruptions. The high dependence on grain corn and soybeans not only puts pressure on production costs, but also makes the country's food supply chain more fragile when faced with international crises. "Malaysia also basically does not have significant local corn and soybean production, so the dependence on imported feed materials remains high.

items.title: Global supply crisis presents opportunities, Malaysia remains safe investment destination – Tengku Zafrul
items.summary: Bernama KUALA LUMPUR: Senior Political Adviser to the Prime Minister Tengku Datuk Seri Zafrul Abdul Aziz said the global supply crisis should be viewed as an opportunity to strengthen Malaysia's position as a safe investment destination. He said Malaysia has a proven track record of economic resilience and a robust investment ecosystem in navigating various crises. Tengku Zafrul said Malaysia must continue positioning itself as a stable and safe investment destination.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia likely to trade sideways between 1,700 and 1,730 next week
items.summary: On the index board, the FBM Top 100 Index gained 198.09 points to 12,549.30, the FBM Emas Index advanced 197.55 points to 12,715.72, the FBM Emas Shariah Index rose 138.55 points to 12,586.61, the FBM ACE Index improved 52.67 points to 4,652.32, while the FBM Mid 70 Index climbed 348.70 points to 18,072.43. Weekly turnover edged up to 16.39 billion units valued at RM15.16 billion from 16.35 billion units valued at RM14.87 billion a week earlier. The Main Market volume expanded to 9.33 billion units valued at RM13.66 billion from 8.97 billion units valued at RM13.41 billion previously.

items.title: Japan inflation cools demand for vending machine drinks
items.summary: AFP TOKYO: From post offices and parks to stations and even the summit of Mount Fuji, Japan's vending machines are ubiquitous. Last month beverage giant DyDo Group Holdings announced it would remove about 20,000 vending machines -- around seven percent of their stock nationwide -- by January 2027, in order to "reconstruct a profitable network". Pokka Sapporo Food & Beverage, based in Nagoya, also said in March it would sell its 40,000-machine operation to Osaka-based Lifedrink Co. "The strength of the vending machine business has been to sell at list prices," a spokeswoman for Pokka Sapporo told AFP.

items.title: Retiree loses home in fire 10 days after husband's death
items.summary: JERANTUT: A retiree, who lost her husband just 10 days ago, was dealt another blow when her house was destroyed in a fire yesterday. The fire destroyed about 80 per cent of the 50-year-old house. "I was in the house when I smelled something odd and saw smoke," said Rusdah Jantan, 61.

items.title: DPM Fadillah calls for institutions, experts to collaborate to document Sarawak’s multi‑ethnic history
items.summary: SIBU, April 26 — Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof has urged experienced individuals and higher education institutions to collaborate in conducting research and documenting the history of Sarawak’s multi-ethnic communities. He said much of this history remains undocumented, particularly aspects related to culture, ethnic origins and the development of the state’s diverse communities. He also stressed the importance of history as a lesson for future generations.

items.title: [UPDATED] Amirudin: Court to decide on Sungai Buloh nursery operators' judicial review bid
items.summary: AMPANG: Selangor Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari has deferred to the courts on a judicial review filed by the Sungai Buloh Green Lane plant nursery operators. Amirudin said that he respects the decisions of the operators as it is their right to bring the matter to court. Amirudin said that the operators had encroached on the land, with discussions spanning several years.

items.title: Early birds queue from 6am for half-price number plates at Batu Kawan carnival
items.summary: BATU KAWAN: Hundreds of eager visitors began lining up as early as 6am today to secure special vehicle registration numbers offered at a 50 per cent discount at the Karnival Jom Heboh (KJH) x Mega JPJ Madani 2026. The strong turnout exceeded expectations, with long queues forming well before sales counters opened at 9am. Road Transport Department director-general Datuk Aedy Fadly Ramli said the first successful buyer, who travelled from Bangi, Selangor, had arrived at dawn to secure a coveted number.

items.title: ‘Serves them right’: Kelantan rep boasts motorcycle convoy used ‘lorong tikus’ to evade police road blocks
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, April 26 — A motorcycle convoy in Kelantan held without the approval of the police was proclaimed a success by the organising assemblyman after its participants used lorong tikus (side streets) to evade the blockades. Melor assemblyman Wan Rohimi Wan Daud, who organised the ‘Konvoi Motosikal dan Lepak Anak Muda Bersama YB YB’, said the programme still proceeded and attracted about 3,000 motorcyclists despite delays caused by police checks, the New Straits Times reported. “When I found out there were many police roadblocks, we used side streets.

items.title: [UDPATED] Selangor to detail flood mitigation efforts in state assembly
items.summary: AMPANG: Selangor will present a detailed response in the state assembly on actions taken to tackle flooding, including engagement of foreign engineers and progress on major projects. Amirudin said that the state has also engaged foreign engineers from the Netherlands for consultancy and delivery works. The project started last year," he said.

items.title: Girl Guides Malaysia sets Asia record for largest camp turnout
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: It was a meaningful anniversary celebration for the Girl Guides Association of Malaysia (PPPM) when the uniformed organisation received recognition from the Asean Records and Asia Records following its largest-ever camp. The achievement was earned through the largest-ever Jade Camp PPPM 2026, titled "Largest Girl Guide Camp and Grand March-In Ceremony At a Stadium Alongside The World's Second Tallest Building For A 110 Year Celebration." Yeoh said the camp served as a meaningful physical gathering point for participants, while fostering new friendships.

items.title: Police tracking down suspected flasher near Dungun university
items.summary: DUNGUN, April 26 — Police are hunting for a man suspected of committing an extreme act of lewdness near the entrance of a public university here recently. Acting Dungun district police chief DSP Jumaidy Bah Chong Weh said his department received a report regarding the incident at 12.30 pm yesterday. He said members of the public with information were asked to come forward to assist in the investigation by contacting the Dungun district police headquarters or any nearby police station.

items.title: 5,900 laid off in March, govt to roll out crisis support measures — Ramanan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Human Resources Ministry will launch a series of targeted crisis support initiatives to address the impact of global economic uncertainty, stemming from ongoing geopolitical tensions. Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan said the move follows a worrying trend in the country's labour market, which saw 5,900 Malaysians laid off in March, with workers in Selangor and Kuala Lumpur the hardest hit. "As a proactive measure by the Madani government, the ministry will announce several comprehensive crisis support initiatives in conjunction with Labour Day on May 1," he said in a statement, after visiting the MYFutureJobs Premium Career Carnival 2026 at the Kuala Lumpur Convention Centre today.

items.title: Ampang azan petition suspect placed on Special Circular List, barred from leaving country, says Selangor police chief
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 26 — The Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) has placed a local man suspected of being involved in the azan petition issue in Ampang on the Special Circular List to prevent him from fleeing the country. Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar said the measure was taken following police reports lodged by residents and members of a surau committee in the area regarding a petition raised in the housing estate. He said that so far, police have recorded statements from several individuals, including representatives of the local community affected by the issue, to help complete the investigation papers.

items.title: 10,000 jobs on offer at Perkeso carnival
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A total of 10,000 job opportunities, with salaries ranging from RM3,000 to RM16,000, were offered at the two-day Social Security Organisation's (Perkeso) MYFutureJobs Premium Carnival 2026, which concluded today. Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan said the carnival featured a wide range of employers, including multinational corporations, government-linked companies (GLCs) and private sector firms. "This carnival will also be held in Borneo, expanding access to employment nationwide," he said after visiting the MYFutureJobs Premium Career Carnival 2026 at the Kuala Lumpur Convention Centre today.

items.title: ‘True face of the Ministry of Health workforce’: Minister salutes Semporna mobile team for mid-sea rescue (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 26 — Health Minister Datuk Seri Dzulkefly Ahmad today praised the Semporna Community Mobile Boat Clinic team for their swift and courageous rescue of two island residents whose boat capsized in Sabah waters last week. “Mr Azalan (Boat Crew): Without hesitation, he immediately jumped into the sea to rescue the victims,” Dzulkefly said in a Facebook post. “Mr Samsi & Mr Ansu: They not only helped secure the victims’ safety, but also saved the boat itself because for residents here, a boat is their lifeline and source of income.

items.title: Student injured after falling into seven‑metre well at Wakaf Bharu school in Kelantan
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, April 26 — A 17-year-old male student sustained head and body injuries after falling into a well more than seven metres deep at Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan Agama Falahiah, Wakaf Bharu, here today. Wakaf Bharu Fire and Rescue Station (BBP) operations commander Azmi Sulaiman said the department received an emergency call at 9.12am, prompting six personnel and a Light Fire Rescue Tender (LFRT) to be dispatched to the scene. “The team arrived about 11 minutes later and found the victim inside the well.

items.title: Remand extended for independent preacher suspected of NGO funds abuse
items.summary: SEREMBAN: The Negri Sembilan Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has obtained a remand extension against an independent preacher on suspicion of embezzling funds from a non-governmental organisation (NGO) totalling about RM12.3 million. The application to extend the remand was made after the original remand period for the suspect expired today. It was previously reported that the state MACC had arrested an independent preacher suspected of abusing power and embezzling funds from an NGO totalling about RM12.3 million for personal gain.

items.title: MACC secures remand extension for preacher in RM12.3m NGO funds probe
items.summary: SEREMBAN, April 26 — The Negeri Sembilan Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has obtained permission to extend the remand of an independent preacher suspected of embezzling funds from a non-governmental organisation (NGO) totalling approximately RM12.3 million. Negeri Sembilan MACC director Awgkok Ahmad Taufik Putra Awg Ismail when contacted confirmed the matter. Previously, the suspect was remanded for five days since last Wednesday and so far, MACC has frozen 15 bank accounts totalling approximately RM16.6 million, comprising four individual accounts, three NGO accounts and eight company accounts.

items.title: Timeline: How the shooting at the White House correspondents' dinner unfolded
items.summary: Soon after, Secret Service agents rushed towards Trump, escorting the president and his cabinet from the ballroom. A sole gunman had rushed a Secret Service checkpoint in a lobby, Washington, DC, Mayor Muriel Bowser and interim police chief Jeffery Carroll later told reporters. As the suspect ran through that checkpoint, Secret Service members intercepted him.

items.title: MOH launches oral health drive, targets older adults to keep 20 teeth by 2030, says Dzulkefly
items.summary: KUALA SELANGOR, April 26 — The Ministry of Health (MOH) is targeting more than 50 per cent of senior citizens to retain at least 20 natural teeth by 2030, in line with the National Oral Health Strategic Plan 2022-2030. Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said that based on the National Oral Health Survey of Adults (NOHSA) 2020, only 34.3 per cent of senior citizens have at least 20 natural teeth, while 13.9 per cent experience oral functional limitations that affect their quality of life. “I have stressed that there is no health without oral health, as it reflects the crucial role of dental health in maintaining overall well-being,” he said when met after launching the 6020 Campaign and the National-level Kembara Senyuman 2026 programme here today.

items.title: Police hunt mother over alleged abuse of three-year-old in Batu Caves
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police are tracking down a woman suspected of abusing her three-year-old son in an incident in Batu Caves recently. Gombak police chief ACP Noor Ariffin Mohamad Nasir said a report was lodged yesterday by the child's grandmother, who lives in the same house. "The suspect is a local woman and the child's biological mother.

items.title: Police seek mother accused of abusing three‑year‑old son in Batu Caves
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 26 — Police are tracking down a woman suspected of abusing her three-year-old son in an incident in Batu Caves recently. Gombak police chief ACP Noor Ariffin Mohamad Nasir said a report was lodged yesterday by the child’s grandmother, who lives in the same house. “The suspect is a local woman and the child’s biological mother.

items.title: Global supply crisis presents opportunities, Malaysia remains safe investment destination, says Tengku Zafrul
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 26 — Senior Political Adviser to the Prime Minister Tengku Datuk Seri Zafrul Abdul Aziz said the global supply crisis should be viewed as an opportunity to strengthen Malaysia’s position as a safe investment destination. He said Malaysia has a proven track record of economic resilience and a robust investment ecosystem in navigating various crises. Tengku Zafrul said Malaysia must continue positioning itself as a stable and safe investment destination.

items.title: Wira Transplant Walk: From dialysis to the summit, patients set sights on Mt Kinabalu climb to spotlight organ donation awareness (VIDEO)
items.summary: Patients on dialysis and organ recipients, alongside doctors and volunteers, are preparing to climb Mt Kinabalu to show that serious illness does not limit active lives. The initiative, Wira Transplant Walk, aims to raise awareness about organ donation and highlight the need for stronger national coordination and a transplant centre in Malaysia. “People still think transplant patients cannot live normally,” he said.

items.title: Sabah Rail to go cashless by June
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 26 — Sabah State Railway (JKNS) is developing a cashless payment system for daily commuters, expected to be completed by June this year. Its director Mohammad Safri Abdul Habi said the initiative is part of the department’s digitalisation efforts, aimed at providing a more convenient and efficient payment method for rail users in the state. “JKNS is finalising the study.

items.title: Ex-judge can improve confidence in MACC says PM’s political adviser
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 26 — The appointment of former High Court Judge Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman to lead the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) can strengthen the people’s confidence in the institution, said senior political adviser to the Prime Minister Tengku Datuk Seri Zafrul Tengku Abdul Aziz Commenting on the matter, Tengku Zafrul said Abdul Halim’s background as a former judge added value to efforts to strengthen the integrity of MACC. “I welcome this appointment. He said the appointment of individuals with integrity would help increase the public’s confidence in efforts to combat corruption.

items.title: As imports eat into Malaysia’s poultry’s feed, food waste and insects offer alternatives
items.summary: The high dependence on grain corn and soybeans not only puts pressure on production costs, but also makes the country’s food supply chain more fragile when faced with international crises. Animal nutrition expert, Prof Dr Hasliza Abu Hassim, said Malaysia currently does not have significant local corn and soybean production, causing the industry to continue to depend on imports. “Malaysia also basically does not have significant local corn and soybean production, so the dependence on imported feed materials remains high.

items.title: Exposed: RM1,000 per head syndicate at Kedah border, senior immigration officer among 10 arrested
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 26 — Ten individuals, including a senior Immigration Department officer, were arrested in an operation dubbed Op Toyu at the Bukit Kayu Hitam Immigration, Customs, Quarantine and Security (ICQS) Complex in Kedah yesterday. Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said the special operation, which began at 12.50 pm, was carried out following a month-long intelligence effort by the Intelligence and Special Operations Division at Immigration headquarters in Putrajaya, in collaboration with the Kedah Immigration Department and the Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (AKPS). “Also detained were five Indian nationals, one Pakistani national and a senior immigration officer believed to have acted as a ‘facilitator’,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Malaysia could capture diverted Hormuz trade flows if logistics efficiency improves, academics say
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 26 — Malaysia is well-positioned to absorb any increase in redirected trade flows through the Strait of Melaka if shippers decide on alternative routes to avoid the conflict-stricken Strait of Hormuz. This is due to the country’s strong geostrategic advantage as a regional supply chain hub, said Universiti Kuala Lumpur professor Dr Nor Aida Abdul Rahman. “The record container volumes handled by Port Klang and Port of Tanjung Pelepas show that Malaysia already has a credible capacity base and market relevance,” she said.

items.title: Four-year-old left by mother melts Putra Perdana caregiver’s heart, adoption process now underway
items.summary: SEPANG, April 26 — A Selangor babysitter with three children of her own is in the process of adopting a four-year-old boy abandoned by his biological mother. Dg Che Nor Hakimah Mudah, in her 30s who lives in Putra Perdana, has been looking after the boy for more than two weeks, Harian Metro reported last night. “I contacted the child’s biological mother on April 10 and she agreed to let me adopt her child,” she was quoted as saying.

items.title: Melaka police move to charge 39-year-old after toddler allegedly tests positive for cannabis-laced sweets
items.summary: MELAKA, April 26 — A 39-year-old man is expected to face charges in court tomorrow for allegedly exposing a three-year-old girl to drug-laced sweets. Melaka police chief Datuk Dzulkhairi Mukhtar said instructions to charge the suspect have been received from the deputy public prosecutor (DPP), Harian Metro reported yesterday. The suspect, who reportedly has a large online following, was arrested on April 15 after the case came to light when the child was treated at Melaka Hospital in a semi-conscious state on April 14.
